summary:
Given an m x n integer matrix, if any cell holds a 0, set every other cell in that cell's row and column to 0 as well.

The update must happen in place. The tricky part is that you must base your zeroing on the original matrix, so a freshly written 0 should not trigger further rows and columns to be cleared.

examples.explanation: The single 0 sits at row 1, column 1, so all of row 1 and all of column 1 become 0.
